bugs.error_message: TvCodePopup: 'Couldn't load the room code — try again in a moment.' Also the original bug-1504 complaint: lobby never showed a tv_code.
bugs.root_cause: normalizeGameSession rebuilds the session object from an explicit field list and didn't include tv_code — the SELECT fetched it, the normalizer silently dropped it. Verified the REST layer returned tv_code fine on both local and prod; the loss was purely client-side mapping. This was the real cause behind bug-1504 (mis-diagnosed as discoverability-only).
bugs.fix: Added tv_code: session.tv_code ?? null to normalizeGameSession + regression test tests/unit/normalize-game-session.test.ts. Pattern to watch: any column added to a game_sessions SELECT must also be added to the normalizer or it vanishes.

bugs.error_message: User: can't test locally anymore 'because of the variables' — app unreachable/failing against Supabase in local dev.
bugs.root_cause: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL pointed at the cloudflared tunnel (supabase.dweemo.nl) but cloudflared wasn't running (Cloudflare 530 origin-unreachable). Local Supabase itself was up on :54321 the whole time. The env vars were fine; the tunnel they pointed at was down.
bugs.fix: Swapped .env.local to the localhost line (http://127.0.0.1:54321) for desktop dev; for phone testing start the tunnel first (cloudflared tunnel run 28b93d77-5d4f-49f1-9d4d-f82ed4637961) and swap back to supabase.dweemo.nl. Diagnostic: curl kong :54321 (200?) vs tunnel host (530 = tunnel down).

bugs.error_message: User: on leylinesync.com/tv the input seems to accept max 5 characters, and the app never shows a TV code to enter.
bugs.root_cause: Not a backend failure (mig 379 applied on hosted Supabase, RPC get_board_access_by_code live, deployed bundle current). The tv_code is only rendered as a tiny text-xs footnote inside the session-detail modal on the home lobby, below the Open controller/board buttons — never on the controller or board views. Secondary: the /tv input has maxLength=6 but its w-56 box at text-4xl + tracking-[0.4em] only shows ~5 characters, so it *looks* capped at 5 (codes are 4 hex chars, so entry itself works).
bugs.fix: Added components/board/TvCodePopup.tsx (fetches tv_code via getGameSession, shows leylinesync.com/tv + the 4-char code huge) + a 📺 button in the controller StatusBar header next to the log/help buttons. Also set the /tv input maxLength 6→4 to match the 4-hex code space, killing the 'max 5 chars' visual clipping.

bugs.error_message: Runtime: 'Oracle meta load failed: URI too long' in loadOracleMeta, triggered by getDashboard with a real (large) collection.
bugs.root_cause: supabase-js `.in('oracle_id', chunk)` serialises every id into the GET URL query string. Chunk size was 300; ~300 uuids made the URL exceed the local Supabase (Kong) gateway URI limit. Only surfaced with real-size collections (tests/e2e used <30 ids).
bugs.fix: Lowered the chunk size to 100 via a shared exported IN_CHUNK const in deck-loader (loadOracleMeta/loadTags/loadBinderNames), resolve.ts NAME_QUERY_CHUNK=100, and upgrade-scanner loadUsedBy. Verified loadOracleMeta with 500 ids loads cleanly. Follow-up: dashboard collection value could be a single SQL aggregate (RPC) instead of loading all owned meta.

bugs.error_message: Deck Conflicts listed basic lands (e.g. 34 Swamps owned 0 / committed 34) as conflicts — noise, since basics are infinitely available.
bugs.root_cause: computeConflicts flags any oracle where committed > owned; a deck's 34 basic lands you don't 'own' as collection rows trips that.
bugs.fix: In listConflicts, after loading oracle meta, drop oracles whose type_line matches /basic land/i before computeConflicts. Same basic-land class of gotcha as bug-1500.

bugs.error_message: Synergy tagger tagged every land (incl. basic Swamp) as 'ramp' — a deck with 34 Swamps showed ramp 34/10, hiding the real ramp need in the power-score/upgrade scanner.
bugs.root_cause: The ramp rule matches 'add {mana}', which also matches a land's intrinsic '{T}: Add {B}' reminder text. Lands tap for mana by definition; that isn't ramp.
bugs.fix: After tagging, delete the 'ramp' tag when the type line includes 'land' (keep the 'land' tag). Land ramp like Cultivate is a nonland card, so it's unaffected. Added regression test.

bugs.error_message: Tagger missed 'removal' on cards worded 'Destroy target nonblack/nonartifact creature' (Doom Blade, Go for the Throat) — removal candidates vanished from the upgrade scanner.
bugs.root_cause: The removal regex required the noun immediately after 'target'; an adjective like 'nonblack' between them broke the match.
bugs.fix: Allow up to 3 adjective words between 'target' and the noun in the removal regex. Added regression tests for Doom Blade + Go for the Throat.

bugs.error_message: ETB triggered abilities authored with event:'fire_etb' are silent no-ops — the engine fires 'enters_the_battlefield'/'etb'/'enters' (fire_zone_change_triggers), never 'fire_etb'. Affected 9 cards across decks incl. already-pushed Edgar cards Champion of Dusk, Dusk Legion Zealot, Cavern of Souls (their ETB effects did nothing on hosted).
bugs.root_cause: The triggered_ability `event` is a free-form z.string() in the schema, so 'fire_etb' validates but matches no fired event. The scions-spellcraft-build workflow's cheat-sheet used 'fire_etb' (an unrelated become_copy parameter name) instead of the real ETB event.
bugs.fix: Replaced all event:'fire_etb' with 'enters_the_battlefield' in card-scripts.json + test-cards.json (9 cards). Edgar cards Champion of Dusk / Dusk Legion Zealot / Cavern of Souls need a re-run of deck:upsert --apply since they were already pushed with the broken event.

The new attacks trigger (migration 077) enqueues a triggered_ability on the stack at declaration; resolving it (pass_priority round) resets priority_player_id to the active player while still in declare_attackers, so the attacker picker re-rendered. Re-declaring the already-declared attacker 400s (unique constraint), and the picker offered no pass affordance -> stuck.
bugs.fix: Compute myDeclaredAttackers = combatAssignments.filter(a => a.attacking_player_id === playerId) and require myDeclaredAttackers.length === 0 in the declare_attackers layoutState condition. Once attackers are declared, fall through to the default layout whose PriorityPanel exposes a Pass button, letting the step advance to declare_blockers.

bugs.error_message: An 'any target' damage trigger was silently dropped when no creature was on the battlefield; and 'destroy target creature an opponent controls' let you destroy your own creature.
bugs.root_cause: Migration 080's trigger_effect_requires_creature_target used behavior_target_type_matches, which returns true for 'any'/['creature','player']. So any-target damage triggers were classified as needing a creature target and dropped by enqueue when no creature existed. Separately, targeted triggers/spells ignored 'an opponent controls' / 'you control' restrictions.
bugs.fix: Added behavior_target_type_is_creature_only (creature-only check) so any-target triggers fall through to apply_triggered_ability_effects' each_opponent default. Added target_controller (any|opponent|you) enforced via session_has_targetable_creature + creature_target_controller_ok in enqueue, choose_triggered_ability_creature_target, resolve_top_of_stack guard, and put_action_on_stack; mirrored client-side (creatureMatchesController/readTargetController filter pickers, SpellPlan.targetController, put-on-stack targetController param).

bugs.error_message: ERROR: relation "public.game_cards" does not exist (SQLSTATE 42P01) during `npx supabase start` / `db reset`, at statement 0 of 202605010000_move_card_to_zone.sql (`update public.game_cards`)
bugs.root_cause: The migration set is incomplete: base tables public.cards, public.game_cards, and public.game_players are never created by any migration (no `create table`, no `alter ... add column`). They were created out-of-band on the hosted project (dashboard/bootstrap), so migrations assume they already exist. A clean local `db reset` from zero has no such tables, so the first migration that touches game_cards fails. Independent of migrations 079-086.
bugs.fix: RESOLVED. A surgical baseline of just the missing tables was NOT enough: the incremental chain also can't replay from scratch (get_session_players/get_stack_items/get_combat_assignments/get_turn_state change return type via create-or-replace -> 42P13; and Supabase keys migrations by leading version digits, so mid-chain drop-shims collide on schema_migrations PK and can't sort between consecutive versions). Final fix = SQUASH for local: `npx supabase db dump --schema public` -> supabase/migrations/00000000000000_baseline.sql (full schema, reproduces prod incl. 079-086); moved the 88 incremental files to supabase/migrations_archive/ (Supabase ignores them, kept as history); added local-only 00000000000001_local_test_relax_fks.sql dropping the auth.users/profiles FK chain so the harness uses throwaway UUIDs. `supabase db reset` now succeeds and the harness proving trio passes.

bugs.error_message: DK5 flaky on a fresh DB: imported commander_card_id != cardId('Goblin Raider Test') (two limit-1 lookups picked different duplicate rows).
bugs.root_cause: node --test runs each test file in its own worker process, so ensureTestCards ran concurrently across ~30 workers. The where-not-exists guard races (workers pass the check before any insert commits) -> 11-13 duplicate rows per test-card name. Most tests tolerate dupes (copies are identical), but DK5 compares the importer's limit-1 pick against cardId()'s independent limit-1 pick -> different duplicate rows -> mismatch.
bugs.fix: Serialise seeding with a transaction-scoped advisory lock (pg_advisory_xact_lock) in ensureTestCards: exactly one worker fills the catalog, the rest wait then find rows present and insert nothing. Rebuilt the DB to clear accumulated dupes (now 0).

bugs.root_cause: resolve_dynamic_amount clamped the literal-number branch with greatest(0, ...), but counter removal (mig 155) relies on NEGATIVE literal amounts in apply_triggered_ability_effects. The mig-161 4-arg resolve_dynamic_amount and the new 5-arg-with-default both matched a 4-arg call, so PostgreSQL refused it as ambiguous. The new test (DA5-8, 5-arg calls) passed; the EXISTING 4-arg callers (apply_triggered_ability_effects) broke â caught only by the full/file suite.
bugs.fix: DROP FUNCTION the old 4-arg signature at the top of the migration before CREATE OR REPLACE of the 5-arg; the 4-arg callers then bind to the 5-arg via the default.

bugs.root_cause: The guided form rendered choose_player's inner effects with caster-centric labels â draw showed 'You draw cards'. But the engine forces every inner effect's recipient to 'controller' and applies them to the CHOSEN player (submit_decision, mig 170), so inside choose_player the subject is the chosen player, not 'you'. bugs.error_message: supabase db push: invalid byte sequence for encoding UTF8: 0x97 (SQLSTATE 22021) applying 202605010202_grant_keyword_all.sql
bugs.root_cause: The migration was composed by a Python heredoc using open(path,'w') WITHOUT encoding= â on Windows that defaults to cp1252, so em-dashes in the header comment were written as byte 0x97 (invalid UTF-8). The local setup script read with Node's 'utf8' (which silently substitutes U+FFFD), so local applies passed; supabase db push sends raw bytes and hosted Postgres rejected them.
bugs.fix: Re-encoded the file cp1252->UTF-8 (em-dashes restored, 0 stray bytes). PREVENTION: scripts/setup-local-test-db.mjs now strict-decodes every SQL file (TextDecoder fatal:true) and fails loudly with the filename before applying; scanned all migrations/functions_src/lib/tests â only this one file was affected. Rule: Python writes on Windows must always pass encoding='utf-8'.

bugs.error_message: bot-runner: connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:54322, then after a restart 'watch error: relation "public.game_session_players" does not exist', then supabase start failing with 'Bind for 0.0.0.0:54322 failed: port is already allocated'.
bugs.root_cause: A SECOND local Supabase project ('Jordy', containers supabase_*_Jordy) was running on the SAME default ports (54321 kong / 54322 db / 54323 studio). It held 54322, so: (1) supabase start for LeylineSync couldn't bind the port; (2) once LeylineSync's db came up half-mapped / the Jordy db held 54322, the bot connected to the WRONG database (Jordy's postgres, which has no LeylineSync tables) -> 'relation does not exist'. Compounded earlier by a Windows WinNAT excluded-port-range (54250-54349 includes 54322) that blocked binding with 'forbidden by its access permissions' until winnat was restarted + 54322 persistently reserved.
bugs.fix: Stop the conflicting project's containers (bash: docker stop $(docker ps -q --filter "name=_Jordy")) so LeylineSync can claim 54321/54322, then npx supabase start. Permanent options: give each project distinct [api]/[db] ports in supabase/config.toml, or always `supabase stop` one project before starting the other. For the WinNAT half: admin PowerShell `net stop winnat; netsh int ipv4 add excludedportrange protocol=tcp startport=54322 numberofports=1 store=persistent; net start winnat`.

bugs.root_cause: Phone PWA loads over the cloudflared https tunnel (app.dweemo.nl) but NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL was http://127.0.0.1:54321 â on the phone 127.0.0.1 is the phone itself, and http from an https page is mixed-content blocked. bugs.fix: Tunnelled local Supabase too: ingress supabase.dweemo.nl -> http://localhost:54321 in ~/.cloudflared/config.yml, DNS route via cloudflared tunnel route dns, Access BYPASS for that hostname, set NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://supabase.dweemo.nl, restarted cloudflared + npm run dev. Access matches the most-specific path, so the bypass app wins for those files while the broad app keeps OTP on the rest.

Downgraded 4 to needs_engine (Olivia's Wrath -X/-X negate, Carmen both triggers, Welcoming Vampire max_power, Immersturm Predator grant/tap self). Added 4 token fixtures (Vampire/Flying Vampire/Eldrazi/Shapeshifter Token) to tests/fixtures/test-cards.json so deck:upsert can create catalog rows.

Added add_mana to UNTARGETED_SPELL_ACTION_TYPES so getSpellPlan returns kind:spell_effect. Harmless Offering: the ENGINE donate already existed (apply_creature_effect.gain_control honors to:opponent, mig 353) but the CAST path was unwired. mig 362 (handle_permanent_effect + build_stack_payload_permanent_simple): builder now accepts kind=gain_control and threads `to`; handle_permanent_effect injects acting_controller=caster for gain_control. Front-end: getSpellPlan routes gain_control to:opponent -> permanent_effect (effectKind gain_control, donate:true, targetController:you) reusing the existing your-permanents picker; castPermanentEffect threads `to`. Tests DR1/HO1/HO2 in tests/feature/donate-and-mana-spell.test.ts. Full suite 1812 pass. NOTE the engine donate picks the lowest-seat opponent of the caster (mig 353); multiplayer caster does not choose which opponent (documented approximation). FOLLOW-UP (mig 363): the multiplayer opponent-choice approximation was removed — donate now parks a choose_player decision (>1 living opponent) so the caster picks the recipient; 1v1 still applies directly. Reusable via params.apply_permanent_effect in submit_decision. Tests HO1/HO2 updated.

bugs.error_message: Long game sessions: network calls pile up over time and memory pressure grows (user: 'na een tijdje tellen de netwerk calls aardig op waardoor de memory vol komt te zitten').
bugs.root_cause: Both active-path hooks ran a 2s setInterval fallback poll whose only guard was realtime SILENCE (Date.now() - lastRealtimeAt >= interval). Turn-based play is idle most of the time, so during think-time the guard always passed and each client reloaded its FULL state every 2s — controller ~18 queries, board ~8 — indefinitely. That is the network pile-up, plus constant re-render/allocation churn. All 12 game tables are in the supabase_realtime publication, so a SUBSCRIBED channel already delivers every change; the silence-poll was redundant.
bugs.fix: Gated the fallback poll on CHANNEL HEALTH instead of silence: track channelHealthy from the .subscribe((status)) callback (SUBSCRIBED → true; CHANNEL_ERROR/TIMED_OUT/CLOSED → false). The interval reloads only while !channelHealthy (channel genuinely down). On a RE-subscribe (hasSubscribedOnce) do one catch-up reload for events missed during the outage. Healthy channel = zero idle polling. tsc + eslint clean.

bugs.error_message: After health-gating the fallback poll, a CPU/bot action produced no UI update on the board (user: bot vuurt waarschijnlijk geen realtime-events).
bugs.root_cause: Supabase realtime postgres_changes is RLS-filtered per subscriber. The board page (app/board/[id]) has no login gate and nothing calls realtime.setAuth, so an unauthenticated board gets a SUBSCRIBED channel that delivers ZERO events (anon RLS denies SELECT). bot-runner writes via raw pg to :54322 and no browser refreshes for a bot turn, so the anon board relied entirely on the 2s fallback poll. The prior health-only gate (poll only if !SUBSCRIBED) trusted the SUBSCRIBED-but-silent anon channel and never polled -> froze on bot/opponent moves. Verified empirically: authed player receives 401 game_cards events from a raw pg write (tunnel + direct); anon receives 0 on all tables.
bugs.fix: Gate the poll on EVENT DELIVERY: poll if (!channelHealthy || !hasEverReceivedEvent) && silent. .on(postgres_changes, onRealtimeEvent) sets hasEverReceivedEvent=true (re-subscribe catch-up calls scheduleReload directly, does not flip it). Authed clients stop idle-polling after first event (network win); anon board keeps polling so bot moves still appear. tsc+eslint clean.

bugs.error_message: Constant heavy fetches even for a logged-in player (fallback poll never stops). Root: realtime postgres_changes deliver zero events to the live browser.
bugs.root_cause: supabase-js _handleTokenChanged calls realtime.setAuth(token) ONLY on SIGNED_IN / TOKEN_REFRESHED / SIGNED_OUT — NOT on INITIAL_SESSION. @supabase/ssr restores the cookie session on every page load AS INITIAL_SESSION, so realtime is never re-authed and stays on the anon apikey. RLS-filtered postgres_changes then deliver 0 events to the logged-in player, so the board/controller never update from realtime and rely entirely on the fallback poll (hasEverReceivedEvent never flips). Proven empirically: anon realtime = 0 events on all tables; setAuth(token) before OR after subscribe = events flow (273 game_cards events).
bugs.fix: In lib/supabase/client.ts, after createBrowserClient, push the restored session token to realtime once: client.auth.getSession().then(({data})=> data.session && client.realtime.setAuth(data.session.access_token)). Guarded by a module flag + window check so it runs once for the browser singleton. Now realtime delivers to logged-in clients -> hasEverReceivedEvent flips -> idle polling stops; anon board still falls back to polling. tsc+eslint clean.

Alisaie registers an affected:'controller' cost_reduction {amount:2, nth_spell:2}. Timing: reduced_mana_cost (charge) runs before the spell_cast increment, so the current cast sees the pre-increment count. Tests: tests/feature/dualcast.test.ts (AL1/AL2 green); 33 cost/cast/watcher regression tests pass.

bugs.error_message: seed-scripts-local --force aborts: "INVALID plain drummer / plain drummer test: triggered_abilities.0.effects.0.type: Known V2 action type with invalid fields" -> 2 invalid, no cards seeded.
bugs.root_cause: The draw action schema required `amount: AmountSchema`, but the engine intentionally defaults a missing amount to 1 ("draw a card") — exactly what the draw-floor DF3 regression guard verifies with a no-amount `{type:draw}` fixture (Plain Drummer Test). The validator was stricter than the engine, so the fixture (and its stripped name) failed validation and aborted the whole local seeder.
bugs.fix: Made draw.amount optional (AmountSchema.optional()) so a no-amount draw is valid, matching engine behaviour. Did NOT touch the fixture (DF3 needs it amount-less). Verified: seeder runs clean (1 filled, no abort); validate:fixtures 366/0 invalid; DF1/DF2/DF3 + curated card-scripts validation all green.

bugs.error_message: Alphinaud Leveilleur's Eukrasia did nothing: 'Whenever you cast your second spell each turn, draw a card.' (found by scanning the Scions Spellcraft FF deck for Nth-spell mechanics).
bugs.root_cause: Only the ETB Partner search was scripted; the Eukrasia second-spell trigger was missing, and the spell_cast watcher had no per-turn spell-count gate.
bugs.fix: Added a `spell_number` filter to fire_watcher_triggers: a spell_cast trigger fires only when resolve_count_amount('spells_cast_this_turn', caster) == spell_number. note_spell_cast (mig 369) runs at the TOP of fire_watcher_triggers so THIS cast is already counted (the 2nd spell reads 2). Added spell_number to the trigger filter Zod schema (else it'd be stripped on seed). Alphinaud script: spell_cast trigger {controller:you, spell_number:2} -> draw 1. Mig 372. Test: tests/feature/second-spell-trigger.test.ts (SS1, library-count isolates the draw); 19 watcher/dualcast regression tests + 367 fixtures green.
